title: Global Network Analyst - Summer Intern
description: At U.S. Bank, we’re on a journey to do our best. Helping the customers and businesses we serve to make better and smarter financial decisions, enabling the communities we support to grow and succeed in the right ways, all more confidently and more often—that’s what we call the courage to thrive. We believe it takes all of us to bring our shared ambition to life, and each person is unique in their potential. A career with U.S. Bank gives you a wide, ever\-growing range of opportunities to discover what makes you thrive. Try new things, learn new skills and discover what you excel at—all from Day One. As a wholly owned subsidiary of U.S. Bank, Elavon is committed to building the platforms and ecosystems that help over 1\.5 million customers around the world to achieve their financial goals—no matter what they need. From transaction processing to customer service, to driving innovation and launching new products, we’re building a range of tailored payment solutions powered by the latest technology. As part of our team, you can explore what motivates and energizes your career goals: partnering with our customers, our communities, and each other. We actively uphold transparent and fair hiring practices that support individual opportunity, inclusive culture, and career mobility across all levels of our organisation. We offer meaningful opportunities for growth, a culture of inclusion, and a strong commitment to transparency and integrity in everything we do. **Job Description** U.S. Bank’s Global Network Management (GNM) team provides centralized governance and oversight of the bank’s global sub‑custodian, cash correspondent, digital asset (crypto), and depositary networks, supporting U.S. Bank’s global custody, fund services, and securities servicing platform. Operating within Wealth, Corporate, Commercial and Institutional Banking (WCIB), the GNM team is responsible for network strategy, market access, due diligence, risk oversight, and ongoing performance management across domestic and international markets. Through close partnership with operations, risk, compliance, legal, tax, and product teams, Global Network Management helps ensure safe, resilient, and compliant market access, consistent service standards, and robust asset‑protection controls across traditional securities, cash, and digital asset ecosystems Our 12\-week paid summer internship program is one of the ways we help early talent thrive at Elavon U.S. Bank. Throughout the summer, you'll have the chance to explore different career pathways, contribute meaningfully to projects across the company and build lasting relationships with teammates, peers and leaders. **Key Responsibilities** * Market \& Industry Research: Research global custody, sub‑custodian banks, cash correspondent networks, and market infrastructure. Summarize key market developments, risks, and trends for the team. * Regulatory \& Market Practice Monitoring: Track regulatory and market practice changes impacting custody and cash networks (e.g., EU and global regulations) and support the preparation of concise impact summaries. * Data \& Reporting Support: Collect and organize data related to sub‑custodian oversight, due diligence, and network performance. Assist with management reports, dashboards, and presentations. * Sub‑Custodian Oversight Support: Assist network managers with due diligence activities, documentation tracking, meeting notes, and follow‑up actions as part of ongoing third‑party risk oversight. * Project \& Managerial Support: Support global network managers with day‑to‑day tasks and ad‑hoc projects related to network governance, market coverage, and process improvements. * Stakeholder Coordination: Help coordinate communication with internal partners (Operations, Risk, Compliance, Legal) and support preparation of materials for external network partners. Being fully immersed in our business will kick\-start your career journey as you learn more about yourself, our culture and the work we do. Our employees come from all kinds of personal and professional backgrounds and each person's career path is a unique journey. As an intern you'll receive: * Impactful, hands\-on experiences that keep you engaged and motivated all summer * Supportive community of peers * Social and volunteer engagements that build meaningful connections * Professional development, leadership exposure and mentoring * Pathway to full\-time offer through our development programs **Who We’re Looking For** We’re looking for intellectually curious and creative thinkers who can think on their feet and thoughtfully react to a changing market. **Core Bahaviours** * Collaboration – Build partnerships and promote a culture of teamwork. * Customer Centricity – Build strong customer relationships and deliver customer centric solutions. * Drive for Results – Hold self and others accountable to consistently achieve meaningful results. * Ethics \& Trust – Maintain high ethical standards; gain the confidence and trust of others through honesty, integrity and authenticity. * Inclusion – Build a culture of equity and inclusion in which diverse ideas, talent and perspectives are welcomed and encouraged. * Risk \- Appropriately identify and manage risks, in compliance with applicable laws, rules and regulations, and with Company Policy. **Qualifications** * Education: Currently enrolled in an undergraduate program, preferably in Finance, Economics, or a related field. * Analytical Skills: Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills with the ability to interpret complex data. * Communication Skills: Excellent written and verbal communication skills. * Attention to Detail: High level of accuracy and attention to detail in all tasks. * Proficiency in MS Office: Familiarity with Microsoft Office Suite, especially Excel and PowerPoint. * Interest in Finance: A genuine interest in pursuing a career in finance, banking, specifically in custody network management. **Preferred Qualifications** * Solid understanding of financial and technology * Strong in Excel and PowerPoint **Location** * Cherrywood . The role offers a hybrid/flexible schedule, which means there's an in\-office expectation of 3 or more days per week and the flexibility to work outside the office location for the other days. **Contract Type:** * 12 week Fixed term Contract This position is not eligible for visa sponsorship **Accessibility** We are committed to providing an inclusive and accessible recruitment experience. If you need adjustments at any stage of the application or hiring process, please contact your recruiter for guidance and support. **Total Rewards** U.S. Bank is committed to fair, equitable, and transparent compensation practices in line with local regulatory and legal requirements. Our total rewards approach is designed to attract, retain, and support top talent while ensuring equal pay for work of equal value. We offer a market\-competitive compensation package that includes: * Clearly defined salary ranges aligned with industry benchmarks and internal equity standards. * Performance\-based incentives for eligible employees (as defined by relevant plan rules), awarded through transparent, objective criteria that recognize both individual and company performance. * Inclusive equitable benefits that are accessible to all employees and focused around our 3 main pillars of financial wellbeing, health \& wellness). * Continuous development opportunities including training, education support, and career progression pathways based on inclusive and transparent criteria. * Employee recognition programs that celebrate achievements and milestones for all. We regularly review our compensation and benefits to ensure they remain competitive, inclusive, and responsive to employee needs and market trends. Further details of the compensation package will be provided upon application. We encourage candidates to explore the full value of our offer, including monetary and non\-monetary benefits, at Employee benefits and development \| U.S. Bank \| Elavon. **Closing Date** Posting may be closed earlier due to high volume of applicants. We aim to provide timely updates throughout the process and encourage early applications to ensure consideration.

title: Graduate Electrical Engineer
description: At Premium Power we challenge and develop our engineers to meet their true capability. We are currently looking for Power Systems Engineers who wish to expand their engineering knowledge and are keen to undertake new challenges. **About us** Premium Power is on a mission to provide best in class electrical engineering consulting on a global scale. We specialize in power systems, electrical safety and arc flash and we collaborate with an outstanding list of household names including Dell, Pfizer, GE and many more. Our ambition is to be the leader in the provision of technology services for electrical energy systems and the optimization of power systems. We’ve made some great strides already and we’ve a lot more to do. Growing our team, we’re looking for a Power Systems Engineer to join us. **Purpose of the Role:** * To work as a power engineer on a range of exciting projects for global clients in a rewarding team environment. * To develop technical and solution development skills to develop into an industry recognised consulting engineer. * The position would suit a practical and adaptable individual who wishes to work in a progressive innovative electrical consultancy. **Key Duties and Responsibilities:** * Learn to conduct power systems analysis using software simulation tools to create an “electrical digital twin” and analyse electrical power system dynamics, transients and protection. * Work with hyperscale data centre and pharmaceutical clients to optimise their electrical system design for maximum reliability and utilising sustainable power sources. * Get trained on developing innovative solution designs. * We will teach you to conduct electrical risk analysis and management. **Typical projects include:** * Strategic power system analysis: redundancy, short circuit, power flow, protection coordination * Electrical safety analysis: arc flash and electrical risk assessments. * Grid connection and embedded generation connections analysis: static \& dynamic transient stability, harmonics, flicker, transformer inrush. * Power quality evaluation: earthing, harmonics, sag ride\-through. * Electrical systems reliability assessments. * Software based analysis solutions. * Supporting wind project and installation teams. * Emerging technology reviews. * Failure investigations and advice. **Experience and Qualifications:** * The applicant should have a Primary (Hons) or Masters degree in Electrical Engineering – Power discipline. * Applicants must have fluent English and be eligible to work in Ireland. * Suitable to graduates with a desire to learn and develop into competent consulting engineer. * Full clean driver’s license and valid passport. * The role will require occasional travel to client sites within Ireland and occasionally abroad. * Above all to show enthusiasm and passion to succeed. * Only candidates who are legally authorized to work in Ireland will be considered. **What we can offer** * Excellent career development, award winning projects and an excellent remuneration package. * High commitment by management to training and career development * Premium Power employees work on interesting projects in a supportive and friendly environment, whilst earning market leading salaries. * Premium Power offers the opportunity to work within an exciting and growing sector on a diverse range of technical, strategic and regulatory consultancy projects for various industries: Data Centres, Pharma, Medical, Semi Conductor, Food \& Beverage, Heavy Industry, etc. * Discretionary annual performance\-based bonus * Flexible working hours * Generous Annual leave entitlements * Company contributory pension scheme * Generous expenses and laptop * Paid membership fees for one professional institute * Excellent CPD programme * Health Insurance Please note applicants must be living in Ireland and have valid work permissions in place in order to apply, sponsorship is not provided. \*PLEASE NOTE – Applications via email will not be considered\* Job Type: Full\-time Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company events * Company pension * Flexitime * On\-site parking * Private medical insurance * Sick pay * Work from home Ability to commute/relocate: * Dublin, County Dublin: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Graduate Engineer
description: We are currently recruiting for a **Graduate Internal Sales Engineer** role with a leading manufacturer of renewable solutions for the commercial, residential, and industrial sectors. **The role:** * It is an office based role, in Dublin 24\. * Work together and learn from HVAC experienced professionals. * Develop your career by working with a leading manufacturer of renewable technologies. * Be part of a dynamic, and motivated team. **Main functions:** * Support the sales network in designing and specifying systems including VRV, Heatpumps, and Fan coil installations. * Support installation of products to specification. * Technical submissions, working with consultants and clients across various project specifications and product compliance requirements. * Generate project based mechanical and electrical schematics for heat pump systems. * Delivering product/project related presentation to customers/clients. * Coordinating internally across departments for total solution projects. **Education:** * We're looking for a recent graduate of Engineering with a focus in design, construction, or renewable technologies who are interested in mechanical HVAC systems or industrial engineering. * Computer literacy \- proficient in MS365 Office \- PowerPoint, Excel, Word, Outlook. * Proficient in CAD/AutoCAD. This role is for an initial 9\-12 months within the graduate position with potential extension to a full\-time permanent basis.

title: Graduate Mechanical Engineers 2026
description: **Position** : Graduate Mechanical Engineers 2026 **Location** : Dublin, Kildare, Cork, Limerick, Gavle, Copenhagen, Groningen, Charleroi **Start date:** We operate on a rolling recruitment basis. Please advise your availability to start in your application form. You can discuss this further with your recruiter upon application. **Company Description** We are a leading engineering services company with a proven track record of high quality, customer focused project delivery for over 130 years. Our core divisions include Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Instrumentation, Sprinkler and Fire Protection. Some of the other services we provide include Waste to Energy (BioEnergy), Specialist Lift, Off Site Fabrication, Technical Support Services, Maintenance, Asset Management, BIM (Building Information Modelling) and Geo\-Surveying. We offer competitive salaries, financial support for further training, Continuous Professional Development (CPD) leading to chartered status, performance incentives and the ability to progress your career with a world class engineering contractor. We have a variety of open roles that can vary from Mechanical Engineer, Quality Assurance/Quality Control Engineer, Project Engineer, Site Engineer \& BIM Engineer. **Job Description** * Liaise with suppliers/specialist contractors for procurement of goods and services * Generation of RFIs, technical submittals, equipment registers and trackers * Assist construction team (Supervisor, subcontractor foreman, QAQC personnel) * Liaise with JEG design team, design consultants and construction management team * Prepare handover packs with QA Dept * Track progress of construction programme / Issue weekly progress reports * Attend weekly construction / design / Permit meetings * Report to Project Manager on project progress * General Admin, document control and site procedures **Requirements** * Relevant third level qualification essential; * Valid Safepass / Manual handling certificates required for successful applicants; * 0\-1 years’ experience post qualification desirable; * Knowledge of a contracting environment would be advantageous; * Ability to work both individually and as part of a multi\-functional team essential; * Excellent IT Skills essential; * Excellent communication and organisation skills essential; **Graduate Development Programme** As part of our commitment to your growth and career development, you will be enrolled in our comprehensive 2\-year Graduate Development Programme. This programme includes experiential learning, mentoring, professional development courses, and in\-house training on key skills such as technical writing, presentation, and time management. You will benefit from peer to peer learning and receive support for obtaining professional certifications. This structured approach ensures you gain the necessary skills and experience to progress in your career. **Benefits** Be part of our Graduate Programme and contribute to meaningful projects that drive innovation and sustainability across multiple industries. We offer a competitive salary package commensurate with experience and qualifications. In addition, you will have the opportunity to work in a dynamic and supportive environment, collaborating with industry leaders and contributing to a sustainable future. Our company values work\-life balance and encourages professional growth through training and development programmes. If you are ready to make a difference and be part of a growing industry, please submit your CV, along with a cover letter highlighting your relevant experience and why you are interested in this role. Recognising the contributions and respecting our people is core to our culture and values. We are an equal opportunities employer, and we encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply for roles. Please note, we are currently not accepting applications from agencies, for this role at this time

title: Graduate Pest Control Technician
description: **Position: Graduate Pest Control Technician** **Location: Dublin 4** **Contract: Permanent, Full Time** **Salary: Base €30,300\.00 Per Annum, paid monthly** **Rentokil Pest Control \- Graduate Pest Control Technician** **Join Our Team and Make a Difference!** **Enjoy problem\-solving and helping customers? Become a Graduate Pest Control Technician with Rentokil in Dublin 4\. Full training provided, no experience necessary. Apply now!** We are currently seeking a **Graduate Pest Control Technician** to join our dedicated team at the **Dublin** branch, covering the **Dublin 4** area. Successful individuals in this role have included graduates with a science\-based, business management or social science degree. If you’re looking for a company that prioritises colleague development, this could be the job for you! **Why Join Rentokil?** * **Competitive Salary Package:** Start with a basic salary of €30,300 per annum, with an increase once fully trained. * **Expected OTE:** €35,000 per annum, with bonus and commission schemes available. * **Benefits:** Company vehicle, fuel card, mobile phone, uniform. * **Relocation Package:** Moving from more than 2 hours away? We may offer up to €5,000 to help you settle in. * **Work\-Life Balance:** Full\-time, permanent role, Monday to Friday (39 hr week), with potential for up to 48 hours in the future with an increased salary. * **Industry\-Leading Training:** Receive top\-notch training to support our customers’ pest control needs. **Graduate Pest Control Technician Role:** You will start as a Pest Control Technician, with your initial 6\-12 months dedicated to covering a specific geographical area. You'll provide invaluable support to customers by resolving their pest control issues while receiving comprehensive industry\-recognised training. Key responsibilities include: * Inspecting customer premises for signs of pests and identifying potential risks. * Swiftly tackling pest problems by cleaning and removing evidence of pest behaviour. * Offering valuable advice to customers on the maintenance and hygiene of their premises. * Completing accurate reports after every visit. * Providing exceptional customer service and upholding a professional image at all times. During the next 12\-36 months, you will have an exciting opportunity to choose your career path from one of the following roles: * Sales Surveyor * Field Biologist * Service Team Leader * Key Account Manager Rentokil offers you the platform to follow your own career path while constantly learning on the job and developing your career. **Requirements** **Graduate Pest Control Technician Requirements:** * A minimum 2\.2 degree in a science/BSc related subject including Social Science, or a BA in Business Management, Hospitality or Tourism. * A full Irish driving licence. * A desire to work hard and have fun while meeting and interacting with customers daily. * Flexibility with working patterns to support business needs. * You may be required to pass a Garda Vetting check depending on the role you have applied for **Benefits** **Benefits:** * Additional opportunities to earn more with regular bonus and commission schemes. * Access to a company vehicle and fuel card. * Salary grading system \- linked to performance for those keen to develop their career within our business. * Opportunity to contribute to a Private Healthcare scheme. * Enrolment in our company pension scheme. * Overtime opportunities \- potential to earn extra in overtime to meet the needs of our customers. * Leads Commission scheme available \- although this isn’t a sales job, we appreciate and reward you for passing on sales leads. * Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) \- free to access and available 24/7 to you, your family, and friends. * Long service recognition \- includes an extra five days of annual leave entitlement after five years of service. * Refer a Friend \- earn up to €1,000 for referring someone to work for Rentokil Initial. **A Company Putting “People First”** Rentokil Initial (FTSE100\) is one of the largest business services companies in the world, operating in over 90 countries and providing services that protect people and enhance lives. Rentokil is the world’s leading commercial pest control services provider, Initial is the world’s leading commercial hygiene services provider, and Ambius is the world’s leading provider of plants and scenting. As a business, we focus on the Right People, doing the Right Things, and in the Right Way. We invest a lot of time and money in training and developing all our colleagues to be the best they can be, and we are always looking for talented and driven people to join our Rentokil Initial Family. If you would like the chance to work in this exciting role for an innovative company, please apply. For more information on the Rentokil Initial family, please call us on \+353 45 850 710 or visit our website https://www.rentokil\-initial.com/ ***Rentokil Initial is an equal opportunities employer and is committed to creating a diverse working environment. To find out how we process your data, view our*** ***privacy policy*** ***here.***

description: **Position:** Graduate Site Engineers / Geospatial Suveyors 2026 **Location** : Dublin, Kildare, Cork, Limerick, Gavle, Copenhagen, Groningen, Charleroi. **Start date:** We operate on a rolling recruitment basis. Please advise your availability to start in your application form. Our core divisions include Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Sprinkler and Fire Protection. Some of the other services we provide include Waste to Energy (BioEnergy), Specialist Lift, Off\-Site Fabrication, Technical Support Services, Maintenance, Asset Management, BIM (Building Information Modelling), and Geo\-Surveying. We offer competitive salaries, financial support for further training, Continuous Professional Development (CPD) leading to chartered status, performance incentives, and the ability to progress your career with a world\-class engineering contractor. **Job Description** As a Graduate Site Engineer / Geospatial Surveyor, you will play a crucial role in ensuring the accurate positioning of mechanical services and equipment using the latest survey technologies and software. You will work closely with a multidisciplinary team of engineers and industry experts to deliver cutting\-edge projects. This role provides opportunities for career progression and development, with full\-site training provided on best\-in\-class instruments and standard industry software. Key Responsibilities * Perform detailed field and office surveying work (Survey Control Network / Laser Scanning / Setting Out). * Prepare and analyse data for construction accuracy. * Utilise modern survey methods and survey devices such as Total Stations and Mobile Laser Scanning devices. * Work with Post processing software such as AutoCAD, Leica Infinity, Navvis Ivion, Register 360, Starnet, Navisworks, etc * Attend Daily/Weekly Meetings with Client and Contractors to discuss project progress and deliver reports. * Any other duties as required by the contractor **Graduate Development Programme** As part of our commitment to your growth and career development, you will be enrolled in our comprehensive 2\-year Graduate Development Programme. This programme includes experiential learning, mentoring, professional development courses, and in\-house training on key skills such as technical writing, presentation, and time management. You will benefit from peer to peer learning and receive support for obtaining professional certifications. This structured approach ensures you gain the necessary skills and experience to progress in your career. **Skills And Requirements** To excel in this role, you should possess the following skills and qualifications: * Degree Qualification in Civil Engineering or Construction Management * Experience: Prior experience would be advantageous but is not essential * Qualifications: Additional qualifications would be advantageous but are not essential * Technological Proficiency: Knowledge of the latest survey technologies and software * Soft Skills: Strong team player with excellent interpersonal skills, organisational, and planning skills * Learning Mindset: Demonstrated appetite for continuous learning and personal development **Benefits** Be part of our Graduate Programme and contribute to meaningful projects that drive innovation and sustainability across multiple industries. We offer a competitive salary package commensurate with experience and qualifications. In addition, you will have the opportunity to work in a dynamic and supportive environment, collaborating with industry leaders and contributing to a sustainable future. Our company values work\-life balance and encourages professional growth through training and development programs. If you are ready to make a difference and be part of a growing industry, please submit your CV, along with a cover letter highlighting your relevant experience and why you are interested in this role. Recognising the contributions and respecting our people is core to our culture and values. We are an equal opportunities employer, and we encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply for roles. Please note, we are currently not accepting applications from agencies, for this role at this time.

title: Graduate Veterinary Nurse - Small Animal
description: **Start Your Veterinary Nursing Journey the Right Way** ***Anicare Veterinary Group \- Blanchardstown, Clontarf or Santry* *Full\-time or Part\-time \| Permanent* *€30,000 (SVN) \- €32,000 (New Graduate RVN)*** **From Student to Skilled Nurse \- With a Team Behind You Every Step of the Way** You’ve worked hard to get here \- late\-night study sessions, long placement days, and a genuine passion for animal care that’s carried you through. Now it’s time to take that next step. At Anicare Veterinary Group, we’re inviting final\-year Student Veterinary Nurses and newly qualified RVNs to join us before or just after graduation, so you can transition into practice with confidence, support, and structure already in place. This isn’t just your first job, it’s your launchpad. A place where you’re encouraged, guided, and given the space to grow into the nurse you’ve trained to become. **Learn By Doing \- Build Confidence From Day One** We know how important those early months are in shaping your career. That’s why we’ve created an environment where learning happens naturally, every single day. You’ll be immersed in all aspects of small animal nursing within a busy, first\-opinion setting \- gaining hands\-on experience in surgical nursing, anaesthesia, inpatient care, and nurse consultations. But just as importantly, you’ll never be expected to figure things out alone. With structured onboarding, approachable mentors, and an experienced team that genuinely wants you to succeed, you’ll be supported to develop at your own pace. Ask questions, try new skills, and gradually take on more responsibility as your confidence grows \- exactly as it should. **Three Clinics, One Shared Purpose: Supporting You** Wherever you join us \- Blanchardstown, Clontarf or Santry \- you’ll find a slightly different environment, but the same supportive culture across the board. In **Blanchardstown** , you’ll work in a newly refurbished clinic with excellent facilities, including a dedicated dental suite, separate operating theatre, and DR x\-ray system. With certificate holders in feline medicine and diagnostic imaging on\-site, it’s an ideal setting if you’re curious, ambitious, and keen to deepen your clinical knowledge early in your career. In **Clontarf,** you’ll become part of a long\-established, collaborative team that genuinely enjoys working together. It’s a great fit if you value camaraderie, shared learning, and being surrounded by experienced colleagues who are invested in your development. In **Santry** , you’ll join a close\-knit nursing team in a well\-equipped first\-opinion clinic. You’ll play an active role in daily practice life \- from assisting with surgeries and monitoring anaesthesia to engaging with clients and helping manage the clinical environment \- building confidence through real responsibility, with support always close at hand. Across all locations, you’ll also benefit from being part of the wider Anicare network \- giving you access to shared knowledge, mentorship, and opportunities across multiple clinics. **A Rota That Supports Your Life (Not Just Your Career)** Starting your career shouldn’t mean burning out. We’ve designed our rota with balance in mind, so you can focus on learning while still enjoying life outside of work. You’ll typically work a 4\-day week, with around 1\.5 weekends in 6, and no out\-of\-hours or overnight commitments. That means you can build your skills in a sustainable, supportive way \- without the pressure of exhausting schedules. We also understand that flexibility matters. Whether you’re looking for part\-time hours or a slightly different working pattern, we’re happy to explore options that work for you. **Who Will Thrive In This Role** This opportunity is perfect for final\-year Student Veterinary Nurses graduating this summer or newly qualified RVNs who are (or soon will be) eligible for VCI registration. More than anything, we’re looking for attitude and enthusiasm. If you’re motivated to learn, enjoy being part of a team, and genuinely care about delivering great patient care, you’ll fit right in. You don’t need to know everything \- that’s what we’re here for. **More Than Your First Job \- A Career That Grows With You** When you join Anicare, you’re not just stepping into a role, you’re joining a pathway. You’ll have access to structured development programmes, including our Graduate Nurse Academy, alongside additional in\-house training and learning opportunities designed to build both your clinical skills and your confidence. As you grow, so do your opportunities. Whether you discover a passion for surgery, inpatient care, client education, or leadership, we’ll support you in exploring and developing your interests over time. **A Team That Feels Like Home** Starting somewhere new can feel daunting, but you won’t feel like “the new person” for long. We pride ourselves on creating friendly, inclusive teams where everyone supports each other and celebrates progress together. From your very first day, you’ll be surrounded by people who remember what it felt like to be in your shoes and are genuinely invested in helping you succeed. **Supporting You, Professionally and Personally** We believe the best nurses are those who feel supported, valued, and able to grow. Alongside a competitive salary, you’ll benefit from a package designed to support your wellbeing and development. **Comprehensive Benefits Package \- Subject To Pro Rata** In addition to a competitive salary, you will also benefit from: **Work life balance** * 22 days annual leave entitlement * Birthday leave **Focus on employee wellbeing** * Paid Maternity, Paternity and Adoptive leave packages * Cycle to work scheme * EAP and wellbeing centre access * Paid bereavement leave \& Company sick pay * Free flu jabs **Development \& Career Growth** * €600 annual CPD allowance with 3 days paid (pro\-rata) * Bespoke Graduate Nurse Academy, Nurse Evolve and many more in\-house academies * Funding of any Certificates / Diplomas / PhD’s you may want to pursue * Leadership Academy for all future leaders, including Head Nurses and Practice Managers * Career progression opportunities **Professional memberships** * VCI membership paid * VDS cover paid **Additional Benefits** * Pawesome benefits \- various discounts including retails and virtual gym * Employee pension contributions * Discounted staff pet veterinary fees * Opportunity to join company programmes (LGBTQIA\+, Wellbeing Champions, Clinical Board and many more) **Ready to Take That First Step?** If you’re about to graduate and looking for a place where you’ll be supported, challenged, and encouraged to grow into the best version of yourself as a veterinary nurse, we’d love to welcome you. Join Anicare as a student nurse now and step confidently into your future as a skilled, capable RVN. We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us. €32,000 per annum

title: Grants Specialist
description: Grants Specialist \- Dublin \- Hybrid ICON plc is a world\-leading healthcare intelligence and clinical research organization. We’re proud to foster an inclusive environment driving innovation and excellence, and we welcome you to join us on our mission to shape the future of clinical development. **Location: Ireland, Dublin** **(office\-based role in ICON Dublin office but 2 home\-based day per week possible)** **The Role** The Investigator Payments Group (IPG) is the investigator payments function for ICON Clinical Research. The department role is to ensure timely, accurate and efficient execution of investigator payments \& to ensure all payments are carried out. This is an excellent opportunity for a self\-starter professional, or even an Accounting or Business Management Graduate, to gain new responsibilities in a range of payments and process driven activities, become commercially focused and be a key player within the organisation. **Responsibilities** * Manage and Process Investigator Payments for their assigned study/studies * Managing the internal inbox and internal system. * Run weekly reports to ensure payments information is accurate. * Collaborate closely with different departments including Clinical, PTS, IT, Finance, Legal, Tax and Contracts, as well as external clients regarding payments. * Review payments in accordance with the investigator site budget and visit date to ensure sites are being paid in an accurate and timely manner * Review all site contracts and set up the proposed payees and budget schedule as governed by the signed Investigator Contract. * To escalate payment delays and/or issues should they arise * Possess excellent system knowledge with the ability to resolve complex issues without support from Team Lead **Experience** * 1 year relevant work experience preferred, or has demonstrated aptitude necessary to execute the role successfully * Excellent customer service skills * Ability to liaise successfully with all levels of Management at ICON, Vendors and Clients * Good working knowledge of PC applications (Excel, Word, Outlook, PowerPoint) * Ability to easily adapt to dynamic environment working to strict deadlines and targets **The benefits of an Account Administrator working at ICON are:** * Flexible working hours as well as remote working. * Free parking on site. * Free onsite Gym. * An excellent opportunity to work in a growing business \& supportive team, that offers steady career support \& progression. Please note we cannot accept applications from candidates on Stamp 1G (graduate or spousal) for this role and we can only consider candidates with a minimum of Stamp 4 or Irish/EU citizenship. **What ICON Can Offer You** Our success depends on the quality of our people. That’s why we’ve made it a priority to build a diverse culture that rewards high performance and nurtures talent. In addition to your competitive salary, ICON offers a range of additional benefits. Our benefits are designed to be competitive within each country and are focused on well\-being and work life balance opportunities for you and your family. **Our Benefits Examples Include** * Various annual leave entitlements * A range of health insurance offerings to suit you and your family’s needs. * Competitive retirement planning offerings to maximize savings and plan with confidence for the years ahead. * Global Employee Assistance Programme, LifeWorks, offering 24\-hour access to a global network of over 80,000 independent specialized professionals who are there to support you and your family’s well\-being. * Life assurance * Flexible country\-specific optional benefits, including childcare vouchers, bike purchase schemes, discounted gym memberships, subsidized travel passes, health assessments, among others. Visit our careers site to read more about the benefits ICON offers. At ICON, inclusion \& belonging are fundamental to our culture and values. We’re dedicated to providing an inclusive and accessible environment for all candidates. ICON is committed to providing a workplace free of discrimination and harassment. All qualified applicants will receive equal consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or protected veteran status. If, because of a medical condition or disability, you need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the application process, or in order to perform the essential functions of a position, please let us know or submit a request here Interested in the role, but unsure if you meet all of the requirements? We would encourage you to apply regardless – there’s every chance you’re exactly what we’re looking for here at ICON whether it is for this or other roles. Are you a current ICON Employee? Please click here to apply

title: HR Information Systems (HRIS) Analyst
description: **Company Description** At EVERSANA®, we are proud to be certified as a Great Place to Work across the globe. We’re fueled by our vision to create a healthier world. How? Our global team of more than 7,000 employees is committed to creating and delivering next\-generation commercialization services to the life sciences industry. We are grounded in our cultural beliefs and serve more than 670 clients ranging from innovative biotech start\-ups to established pharmaceutical companies. Our products, services and solutions help bring innovative therapies to market and support the patients who depend on them. Our jobs, skills and talents are unique, but together we make an impact every day. Join us! Across our growing organization, we embrace diversity in backgrounds and experiences. Improving patient lives around the world is a priority, and we need people from all backgrounds and swaths of life to help build the future of the healthcare and the life sciences industry. We believe our people make all the difference in cultivating an inclusive culture that embraces our cultural beliefs. We are deliberate and self\-reflective about the kind of team and culture we are building. We look for team members that are not only strong in their own aptitudes but also who care deeply about EVERSANA, our people, clients and most importantly, the patients we serve. We are EVERSANA. **Job Description** **THE POSITION****:** The UKG Pro HRIS Analyst is responsible for supporting, maintaining, and optimizing EVERSANA’s UKG Pro HCM system across HR, Payroll, Benefits, Talent, and Onboarding modules. This role serves as a subject matter expert for system configuration, troubleshooting, and end‑user support. The position supports HRIS processes for EVERSANA’s global workforce while leading small projects and system enhancements that strengthen system reliability and enable a seamless and consistent employee experience. This role will partner with HR, Payroll, IT, and business stakeholders to ensure data accuracy, system efficiency, and highly reliable employee experience. This position will identify, research, track, and ensure timely resolution or appropriate escalation of technical issues (internal user support \& external vendor support). **This role will overlap US hours. Your working day will start between 11am to 12pm** **ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:** As a HRIS Analyst, you will collaborate with stakeholders across the business, HR, IT, Finance, and Payroll to understand the business needs and to deliver effective, end‑to‑end global HCM process and technology solutions. **System Administration \& Support** * Serve as the primary support resource for UKG Pro configuration, troubleshooting, and user inquiries. * Maintain foundational system setup including business rules, workflows, security roles, and data tables. * Monitor system performance and proactively identify opportunities for improvement. * Develop and maintain documentation for processes, configurations, and standard operating procedures. * Lead small‑scale HRIS projects and system enhancements from initial requirements gathering through testing, deployment, and post‑implementation support. **Workflow \& Security** * Create, update, and optimize UKG Pro workflows (e.g., onboarding, job changes, manager self\-service). * Maintain role\-based access security; perform monthly access audits and ensure least privilege standards. * Collaborate with IT to maintain secure integration and timely user provisioning/deprovisioning. **Testing, Releases \& Enhancements** * Review monthly/quarterly UKG release notes; assess impacts and test updates. * Participate in system enhancement cycles, UAT testing, and change management efforts. * Recommend configuration improvements that streamline HR processes and enhance user experience. **Cross\-Functional Collaboration** * Partner with HR, Payroll, Benefits, Finance, and IT to ensure system alignment with business needs. * Support annual cycles such as Open Enrollment, Year\-End Payroll, Performance, and Compensation processes. * Provide training and guidance to HR and business partners to maximize use of UKG Pro functionality. * Demonstrate a commitment to diversity, equity, and inclusion through continuous development, modeling inclusive behaviors, and proactively managing bias. * All other duties as assigned. **EXPECTIONS OF THE JOB:** * **Travel** (as needed) * **Hours** (40 per week with occasional overage to meet critical deadlines/resolve critical issues, M \- F). **This role will overlap US hours. Your working day will start between 11am to 12pm** **Qualifications** **MINIMUM KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES:** The requirements listed below are representative of the experience, education, knowledge, skill and/or abilities required. * **Education** Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources, Finance, Business Administration, Computer Science, Information Systems or a related field. Equivalent work experience may substitute for the degree requirement**.** * **Experience and/or Training** + 3–5 years of hands\-on experience with UKG Pro (Core HCM required; experience with Payroll, Benefits, Onboarding, or Talent preferred) + Strong understanding of HR processes, U.S. employment practices, and payroll fundamentals. + Familiarity with HR compliance requirements such as FLSA, FMLA, ACA, and payroll tax laws. + Demonstrated experience supporting and optimizing HRIS processes, including configuration, troubleshooting, and compliance alignment, across global regions, ensuring consistency while adapting to local regulatory requirements. + Experience with workflow configuration, system audits, and role\-based security management. + Hands\-on UKG Pro configuration and system administration experience, including management of system settings, workflow routing, Business Process Flows (BPFs), role\-based security, module configuration (Core HR, Benefits, Payroll, Talent, etc.), and participation in testing and validation during system releases or upgrades. + Ability to analyze data, problem\-solve, and clearly communicate technical information. + Experience in HR systems architecture with sound understanding of data, functional interactions, and impacts to downstream systems. + Experience supporting integrations via API, SFTP, or vendor feeds. + UKG Pro certifications or completion of UKG training modules. * **Technology/Equipment** + Experience with Payroll processes and technology + Exposure to project management or HR system implementation cycles. + Intermediate to advanced skills in UKG Pro Reporting/BI tools. **Additional Information** **Patient Minded**I act with the patient’s best interest in mind. **Client Delight**I own every client experience and its impact on results. **Take Action**I am empowered and hold myself accountable. **Embrace Diversity**I create an environment of awareness and respect. **Grow Talent**I own my development and invest in the development of others. **Win Together**I passionately connect with anyone, anywhere, anytime to achieve results. **Communication Matters**I speak up to create transparent, thoughtful, and timely dialogue. **Always Innovate** I am bold and creative in everything I do. Our team is aware of recent fraudulent job offers in the market, misrepresenting EVERSANA. Recruitment fraud is a sophisticated scam commonly perpetrated through online services using fake websites, unsolicited e\-mails, or even text messages claiming to be a legitimate company. Some of these scams request personal information and even payment for training or job application fees. Please know EVERSANA would never require personal information nor payment of any kind during the employment process. We respect the personal rights of all candidates looking to explore careers at EVERSANA. From EVERSANA’s inception, Diversity, Equity \& Inclusion have always been key to our success. We are an Equal Opportunity Employer, and our employees are people with different strengths, experiences, and backgrounds who share a passion for improving the lives of patients and leading innovation within the healthcare industry. Diversity not only includes race and gender identity, but also age, disability status, veteran status, sexual orientation, religion, and many other parts of one’s identity. All of our employees’ points of view are key to our success, and inclusion is everyone's responsibility. Follow us on LinkedIn \| Twitter

title: Horticulture Internship
description: Dublin Zoo holds the prestigious position of being one of Ireland's top Family Attractions for almost 200 years. We are a conservation charity. Our purpose is to save wildlife and inspire a passion for nature \- one person, one family at a time. All members of the Dublin Zoo team are expected to be champions of our CARE Values: we Care, we are A Team, we are Respectful and we are Excellent. The plants in Dublin Zoo are an essential part of the landscape, simulating the habitat of various animals, and providing an immersive experience for our visitors \- a journey to discover realistic settings of particular habitats. We are currently seeking a highly motivated **Horticulture Intern** to join our Horticultural team on a **12\-week full\-time internship**. The successful candidate will gain valuable hands\-on experience and expertise in the horticultural field while supporting the services provided by the Zoo. This is an excellent opportunity for students or recent graduates to develop their skills in a unique zoological environment. **Role Overview** The intern will support the team in maintaining the green appearance of Dublin Zoo, ensuring that the Zoo has a wide variety of plants and care for their good condition. The Horticulture Team deals with diverse challenges from poisonous plant management, soil compaction issues, visitor questions to providing educational materials, apart from the demands of maintaining and developing a very diverse range of plants. The role involves regular activities such as planting, pruning, weeding, monitoring plants and learning to use necessary machinery under supervision. **Requirements** **Essential** * Basic knowledge and understanding of general gardening techniques, especially weeding and pruning * Some experience in gardening (personal, academic, or professional) * Currently studying horticulture, botany, or other relevant field OR recent graduate * Punctual in attendance, positive attitude, motivated, eager to learn * Demonstrated interest in horticultural/gardening practices * Able to work as part of a team **Desired** * Experience working in a zoological garden or botanical garden * Familiarity with horticultural machinery * Previous internship or work experience in horticulture **Internship Details** * **Duration:** 12 weeks * **Schedule:** Full\-time * **Compensation:** €14\.15 per hour * **Type:** Internship/Work Experience This internship offers an exceptional opportunity to gain practical experience in zoological horticulture while contributing to one of Ireland's most beloved attractions. Job Type: Internship Contract length: 12 weeks Pay: €14\.15 per hour Benefits: * On\-site parking Experience: * Gardening: 1 year (preferred) Work Location: In person

title: Housekeeper - Ireland / 7381 - Keavan`s Port Hotel, Camden Street
description: The ideal applicant We're looking for fun, enthusiastic, passionate people to join our hard\-working teams. No experience is needed, just a good attitude, a willingness to learn and a calm head under pressure. As a UK Top Employer with an award\-winning training programme, we know that we can teach you the skills you'll need to succeed. Whether you're looking for a long\-term career or just some extra shifts, if you're reliable, friendly and love working as part of a team \- this role could be perfect for you. This role is not suitable for under\-18s because of lone working. There may be other roles available, including front\-of\-house and kitchen roles. What's in it for you? * competitive rate of pay * a free meal and a drink, when working * 20% discount on all food, drinks and hotel accommodation (for you and up to three guests), when not working * €1 extra per hour, for hours worked during midnight\-5\.59am * bonus scheme \- earn up to 19% of your pay * availability of guaranteed\-hours contracts and variable\-hours contracts * paid holiday * free shares (after a qualifying period) * loyalty reward scheme The role At a glance, your role is to clean all areas of the hotel thoroughly, with the aim of enhancing the guests' experience by having excellent standards of cleanliness at all times. **Duties include:** * interacting politely with guests \- greeting them, saying 'thank you' and 'goodbye' * servicing bedrooms and all other areas of the hotel in line with our standard operating procedures * completing daily, weekly and monthly cleaning tasks, as required * following correct procedures when using cleaning chemicals and equipment * monitoring cleaning supplies and notifying the manager when more are needed * storing all laundry correctly and completing relevant documentation * adhering to stock\-control procedures and maintaining security of stock at all times * reporting any security issues, safety hazards, accidents, incidents and/or maintenance issues to the manager on duty * completing required induction, role\-specific and ongoing refresher training and contributing to continuous improvement Progression and development Most Wetherspoon team leaders and managers started as an associate. If you're looking to progress, we can support your development with: * structured on\-the\-job training. * apprenticeships and management academies. * cross\-training into other areas (bar, floor, kitchen). * opportunities to progress into team\-leader and management roles and beyond. Wetherspoon promotes an inclusive working environment where everyone is welcome. When you're at work, we want you to feel comfortable being yourself. Whatever your background, preferences or beliefs, we'll ensure that you have the skills and knowledge you need for a great career with us. If a high number of applications is received, this vacancy may close early. **HOURS PER WEEK:** 30

title: HubSpot Solutions Strategist
description: Salted Stone is a fast\-paced, full\-service digital marketing agency, and we are looking for a **HubSpot Solutions Strategist** to join our growing team. Our mission is to empower growth\-minded companies through creative solutions combined with smart technology. We believe nothing is impossible and always solve for the customer. We aim to attract, engage, and develop exceptional people and, in turn, deliver distinctive, lasting improvements for our customers. This is a mid\-level full\-time position reporting to the Director of Solutions on our Solutions Architecture team (commonly known as Technical Marketing elsewhere). The HubSpot Solutions Strategist will help our clients develop, implement, and optimize new technologies and processes that tie together the entire customer lifecycle using the HubSpot platform. The HubSpot Solutions Strategist supports the solutions team by cultivating professional expertise and applying this to address a range of technical marketing, sales, and service issues. They determine appropriate courses of action based on established guidelines and adapt processes and methods as necessary. Ideal candidates enjoy working directly with our clients and internal team to lead engagements from a technical standpoint (think: ideation up to execution), are organized, great communicators, and aren’t afraid to tackle challenges head\-on! **Job Responsibilities** * Complete and deliver all elements of the Salted Stones Solutions Team Product offerings (Data Migration, Portal Strategy, and HubSpot Onboarding including Marketing Hub, Sales Hub, Service Hub, etc.) * Interact directly with and provide a translation layer to technically\-oriented marketers and software developers, internally and on the client\-side. * Lead client\-facing calls, providing technical expertise and a complete understanding of tools to help clients understand and use offerings effectively. * Run solutions onboarding engagements (Marketing Hub, Sales Hub, and/or Service Hub) without relying on CS team members to run points/project management. * Work closely with the development team (both internally and externally) to connect existing systems to HubSpot. * Be able to generate a standard ERD for either existing or proposed integration structure. * Leverage understanding of data structures to provide recommendations and implementation of systems setup. * Be able to discuss 1st party, 3rd party, and Middleware integration solutions. * Troubleshoot integration and data problems. * Regularly thinks creatively on how to use the HubSpot Platform to solve for client needs. * Develop client\-facing supporting documentation and processes for new technology configurations, customizations, and functionalities. * Execute assigned tasks with a high degree of quality and on time. * Provide accurate time estimates for your work, work within timelines and budgets, and other applicable project management concerns. * Contribute to the continuous improvement of the team by identifying areas for optimization, streamlining processes, and implementing innovative solutions. * Lead pre\-sales engagements from discovery through solution design, translating client business objectives into scalable, technically sound HubSpot strategies and clearly articulating value, approach, and scope to both technical and non\-technical stakeholders. * Deliver tailored solution demonstrations that effectively articulate the value of HubSpot and proposed architectures, aligning platform capabilities to client\-specific requirements, use cases, and desired business outcomes. **Required Competencies** * + Successful experience in a Solutions Strategy, Technical Marketing, Marketing/Revenue Technologist, Marketing/Revenue Operations, etc., role (agency experience a plus); ideally suited for early to mid\-career level, but not entry level. + Proven experience with HubSpot (Sales Hub, Marketing Hub, Service Hub). + Excellent organizational skills and the ability to drive and participate in multiple client engagements at once. + Strong technical writing skills \& documentation skills, primarily for creation of compelling client\-facing deliverables. + Ability to confidently communicate with clients as needed. + Excellent project management, organizational and time management skills; and you're comfortable managing multiple projects (budgets, timelines, personalities) simultaneously. + Understanding and/or direct experience with Google Analytics, Databox, and similar analytics tools. + Understanding and/or direct experience with Google Tag Manager. + Basic knowledge of marketing and sales automation tools, business intelligence tools, data enrichment tools, etc. + Strong understanding of sales processes and CRM lifecycle management (pipeline design, lead lifecycle, attribution basics) Experience contributing to or supporting proposal/RFP responses and statements of work (SOWs). + Ability to lead structured discovery sessions, gathering and synthesizing business, technical, and data requirements. + Demonstrated consultative mindset, with the ability to challenge client assumptions and guide toward better solutions. **S2 General Requirements** * **Team player.**Friendly, authentic, collaborative, and makes an effort with relationships (the S2 team, clients, HubSpot). Shows care and respect for those around them and themselves. Live our Values. * **Growth mindset.** Prioritises learning and self\-improvement. Shows motivation and dedication to growth, personally and professionally. * **Agile**. Adapts to changing priorities and conditions, complexity, and change. * **Proactive**. Demonstrates tenacity and acts without being told what to do. Brings new ideas to S2\. Finds solutions and opportunities, not just problems. * **Prioritizes communication**\- Clear, transparent, and consistent with communication across your team and manager, with other teams, and with clients. If in doubt, over\-communicates. Isn’t afraid to call out and help correct mistakes before they impact the team or the business. * **HubSpot\-centric.** HubSpot is at the center of most work at Salted Stone. Not everyone has to be a HubSpot expert to work here, but everyone needs to have a baseline interest and understanding. **You’ll enjoy working:** In a hybrid role \- our team chose the balance of remote work and contact time in our Dublin office that best suits them and their roles. They’re split between Ireland and Perth, Australia so are used to doing things on Zoom and async. S2’s wider team is spread across North America, Australia, Ireland, and the Philippines so there may be times that we ask you to flex your schedule to allow for collaboration with different timezones. This flex goes both ways. **Benefits** Our Irish team enjoy: * Company paid health insurance and pension match * Birthday and Anniversary Leave (an additional paid day off) * Holisticly wellness benefit * Regular Culture Committee initiatives \& events * Cycle to Work Scheme * Flexible, Hybrid or Remote Work * Team Lunches \& Happy Hours * Company provided laptop **Application process** * Resume review * Initial call with the Recruitment Team * Full interview with the hiring Director \& Regional Director, EMEA * Practicum for select applicants * Reference checking

title: IAM Operations Engineer
description: **IAM Operations Engineer** The Dell Security \& Resiliency organization (SRO) manages the security risk across all aspects of Dell’s business. You will have an excellent opportunity to influence the security culture at Dell and further develop your career. Join us as an **IAM Operations Engineer** on our **Cybersecurity team** in **Limerick, Dublin or Cork** to do the best work of your career and make a profound social impact. **What you’ll achieve** As an IAM Operations Engineer, you will be responsible for the operational support, optimization, planning, design, testing, and management of Identity \& Access Management (IAM) tools and technologies. You will play a critical part in ensuring secure, reliable identity services across the enterprise by executing daily operational activities, contributing to design discussions, leading troubleshooting efforts, and supporting incident response workflows. Working as part of a global team, you will help to ensure consistent operational execution, maintain strong technical documentation, improve IAM processes, and collaborate with cyber engineering, IT, and business application teams to enhance the organization’s identity security posture. **You will:** * Oversee Incident, Change, Problem, and Knowledge Management processes and troubleshoot IAM tool and process issues such as sync and attribute errors, provisioning failures, authentication issues, and access workflow errors * Create and update detailed documentation (such as runbooks, SOPs, process flows, and knowledge articles) to support daily operations, cybersecurity programs, compliance needs, and audit readiness * Maintain and optimize tools, workflows, processes, reporting, and automation opportunities * Participate in tool and process reviews to identify improvements and evaluate emerging IAM technologies * Support upgrades, patches, migrations, and new integrations by creating and running test plans to ensure IAM capabilities function correctly and meet security requirements **Take the first step towards your dream career** Every Dell Technologies team member brings something unique to the table. Here’s what we are looking for with this role: **Essential Requirements** * 3\+ years of experience in IAM operations, IT operations, cybersecurity, or a related technical field * Hands\-on experience with IAM platforms, such as: + Directory services (Active Directory, Entra ID) + Authentication \& MFA platforms + Identity governance or access management tools * Working knowledge of IAM concepts (JML processes, RBAC, least privilege, access governance, SSO) * Strong technical troubleshooting and problem\-solving skills * Strong communication skills, including English – written and spoken **Desirable Requirements** * Experience supporting or configuring IAM tools such as SailPoint, Okta, CyberArk, Ping, or similar platforms * Familiarity with ITIL processes (Incident, Change, Problem Management) * Experience with security operations workflows or SOC collaboration **Who we are** We believe that each of us has the power to make an impact. That’s why we put our team members at the center of everything we do. If you’re looking for an opportunity to grow your career with some of the best minds and most advanced tech in the industry, we’re looking for you. Dell Technologies is a unique family of businesses that helps individuals and organizations transform how they work, live and play. Join us to build a future that works for everyone because Progress Takes All of Us. **Application closing date: 29th May 2026 \-** Role may close sooner Apply Now! Dell Technologies is committed to the principle equal employment opportunity for all employees and to provide employees with a work environment free of discrimination and harassment. Read the full Equal Employment Opportunity Policy here.

title: IT Operations Engineer
description: **What’s the Opportunity** We are seeking a skilled and proactive IT Operations Engineer to join our team. In this critical role, you will be instrumental in delivering IT support to Distilled’s Employees enabling a seamless hybrid working environment and ensuring everyone has the tools and support needed to do their best work, while maintaining the smooth operation and continuous improvement of our internal IT systems. You'll leverage your expertise to resolve complex technical challenges, contribute to strategic IT initiatives and work with a diverse range of cutting\-edge technologies that drive our company's success. This is an exciting opportunity to deepen your technical knowledge, enhance our operational efficiency, and play a key role in scaling our IT environment. **Location:** This role requires working from our Dublin office 4 days per week. **What will I be doing** As an IT Operations Engineer, you, along with the existing ITOps team, will be responsible for a wide range of technical operations, ensuring the delivery of dependable and resilient IT services. The ideal candidate can balance reactive support—managing urgent incoming issues—with proactive work such as scheduled maintenance and strategic improvements, while consistently looking for opportunities to optimize systems \& processes and to ensure that our employees have the tools to do their best work. * **IT Support \& Service Delivery:** Deliver comprehensive remote and onsite IT support through the ticketing system for approximately 250 employees across multiple locations and countries, with a strong focus on providing an excellent service experience for all users. Responsibilities include troubleshooting hardware, software, and system issues, managing equipment lifecycle and procurement, and overseeing the user lifecycle, including onboarding and offboarding. The role also requires a proactive approach to anticipating user needs and continuously improving the support experience. * **Infrastructure Administration:** Administer, monitor and optimize critical IT infrastructure, including networking, firewalls, video conferencing and AV systems, ensuring performance, security and high availability. Manage and secure remote devices via Jamf, Intune and Google Workspace * **Identity \& Access Management:** Manage user accounts and permissions within Okta/Active Directory, encompassing security policy and MFA administration. Optimise the IT aspects of employee onboarding and offboarding processes. * **Operational Excellence \& Documentation:** Drive continuous improvement initiatives for IT operations. Create and maintain comprehensive IT documentation. Co\-manage IT asset inventory and refine escalation processes. * **Project Contribution \& Event Support:** Contribute to the planning and implementation phases of IT projects with a focus on innovation, automation and security. Provide technical support for company\-wide initiatives and events. **What skills and experience do I need** * At least 3 years experience in an IT Operations or System Administration role providing hands on support for employees. * Extensive enterprise experience administering and troubleshooting macOS, Windows, and mobile devices, with strong expertise in MDM solutions including Intune, Jamf and Google Workspace. * Proven expertise in administering and supporting Google Workspace or Office 365\. * Hands\-on experience with administering Cloud Computing platforms (AWS/Azure/ Google Cloud), including IaaS and PaaS components. * Experience in managing and troubleshooting enterprise\-level Video Conferencing systems. * Solid understanding and practical experience with Networking concepts (VPNs, Firewalls, Wi\-Fi, VLANs, routing, switching). * Experience with Identity Management platforms (e.g., Okta, Azure AD Identity Protection, SSO integration). * GDPR and information security focused. * A proactive, adaptable professional with outstanding interpersonal and communication skills, as well as a technical and problem\-solving mindset. **Advantage if you have:** * Experience with scripting languages for automation and system administration. * Familiarity with IT security best practices and incident response. * Industry recognised credentials: Microsoft Azure/Endpoint/SecOps, AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner, Okta Professional/Administrator, Jamf 100/170/300 , Associate Google Workspace Administrator, Comptia Security\+, ITIL Foundation. * Experience in upcoming AI trends in predictive security, agentic AI for automation and specialized IT service management (ITSM) to handle AI\-driven threats **What’s it like to work here** You will have the opportunity to work with one of Ireland's fastest growing Internet companies. You will get the chance to create a significant contribution to our strategic direction. Projects are exciting and employees are given wide scope for creativity and innovation **Benefits \- what you can expect** * Our goal is to maintain a culture where people look forward to work – whether in our centrally located office in Dublin or working from home. * We do that by making Distilled a place you'll be listened to and trusted to do your job. Where you can have fun, learn and feel appreciated. And where you get focused career development, growth and progression opportunities. * We want you to feel confident that this is the right place for you, your career, your wellbeing and your future. * Above all, your health and wellbeing are at the forefront of how we build the business. That means looking after you while you're here and later in life. So you'll receive life insurance, fully\-paid healthcare and a 5% contribution towards your pension. **More about Distilled** We exist to make buying and selling in Ireland simple. We put our people first, creating an inclusive environment where we can develop products that put our users first. We value people’s work and time, creating a community where they have an opportunity to make an impact and grow. Our challenge is to enhance our market\-leading products using insights and empathy to present our users with the perfect match. What we build is technology, but what we do is create connections for life’s most meaningful moments. We are Distilled and we **bring people together**. **Our Values** We have five core values: We have a **growth mindset.** We like to **simplify things** and **make things happen**. We **collaborate** across teams, business units and brands. We're **passionate** about what we do. These values give us a framework for leadership and daily decision making. They embody how we behave and present ourselves — as individuals and as a business — both internally and externally for our colleagues, our users and our customers. *We are a diverse bunch of people here in Distilled, and we want to continue to attract and retain a diverse range of people into our Company. We do not discriminate based on gender, marital status, family status, sexual orientation, religion, age, disability, race and membership of the Traveller community.*

title: IT Systems Administrator | Public Sector

Online, OneDrive for Business, Teams, Intune, Power Platform, Power BI, and related applications. **The role will have the following responsibilities:** * Oversee the management of multiple Microsoft 365 tenants, including Exchange Online, SharePoint Online, OneDrive for Business, Teams, Intune, Power Platform, Power BI, and related applications. * Supervise Active Directory/ADFS on\-premise, Group Policy Management, Azure AD (Entra ID) infrastructure, Exchange on\-prem, and SharePoint on\-prem. * Develop and implement automation strategies using AI, PowerShell and Power Platform, Logic Apps to streamline processes. * Ensure the maintenance of key corporate and program\-related systems. * Oversee device management and onboarding processes using Microsoft Endpoint Manager (Intune). * Lead the implementation of new solutions, including migrations from on\- * Provide Level 3 technical support for critical applications within the ICT escalation process for end users. * Utilize call logging software to document and resolve user incidents within specified * Diagnose and troubleshoot complex issues using advanced technical expertise, product knowledge, communication, and problem\-solving skills. * Serve as an escalation point for the ICT Service Desk/Support Teams for logged cases/ * Monitor, verify, and maintain daily processes, including logs, alerts, and maintenance * Support the development of knowledge across operational teams through training and * Understand the impacts of change and support teams through transitions by analyzing needs and engaging with relevant stakeholders. * Lead initiatives to improve service delivery and operational * Collaborate closely with different infrastructure and business **Interested applicants will meet the following requirements:** * A relevant third level qualification is desirable. * 5\-10 years of experience in backend systems operations, IT infrastructure, and operational support. * Advanced certifications in Microsoft 365, Entra ID, Active Directory or related technologies are highly desirable. * Excellent communication, report writing, organisational and interpersonal skills. * Strong team leadership and management skills. * Strong stakeholder management skills. * Ability to embrace change by being flexible and enthusiastic to new ideas. This is a permanent position with a salary scale on offer in the region of €70,000 \- €75,000 plus benefits depending on experience. To be considered for this opportunity, please send an up\-to\-date version of your cv for review to Laura Craughwell at Staffline Recruitment via the enclosed link. **Desired Skills and Experience** Power BI, Microsoft 355, Powershell, scripting, sharepoint, Intune, Power Platform, public sector, non profit, Azure ad, Microsoft Endpoint Manager, OneDrive for Business, Exchange Online, level 3 support, project management, active Directory

title: Inside Sales Executive - Airbnb (French Bilingual)
